channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Hegseth confronts allegations of misconduct as senators grill Trump's choice for Pentagon chief
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump's choice for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, vowed Tuesday to foster a “warrior culture” at the Pentagon and confronted allegations of sexual assault and excessive drinking and questions about his derisive views of women in combat during a heated Senat
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, TARA COPP and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dem senator ripped for invoking Hegseth's young daughter in 'despicable' confirmation hearing exchange
item.description: SecDef nominee Pete Hegseth and Dem. Sen. Tim Kaine engaged in a tense back and forth about Hegseth's marital history and Hegseth's young daughter was mentioned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk poised to occupy White House office space: report
item.description: Elon Musk could occupy space in the Eisenhower Executive Office Building next to the West Wing to head up the newly created Department of Government Efficiency, known as DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Michelle Obama's absence was notable given her tough past criticism of Trump, who succeeded her husband in office.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth interrupted by multiple protesters during Senate confirmation hearing
item.description: Secretary of Defense nominee Pete Hegseth's confirmation hearing before the Senate Armed Services Committee was interrupted by protesters on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Speaker Johnson orders US Capitol flags raised to full height for Trump's inauguration
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson has ordered that flags at the U.S. Capitol be raised to their full height on Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration, 11 days after missing Carter funeral
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama will not attend the inauguration of President-elect Trump on Monday, the second time in two weeks that she will be absent from a significant event.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats' hopes of derailing Trump nominees fading fast
item.description: Democrats' hopes of defeating any of President-elect Trump's Cabinet nominees appear to be fizzling as Senate committees prepare for the first week of hearings.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: No. 2 Senate Democratic leader will back Rubio for secretary of State
item.description: Senate Democratic Whip Dick Durbin (Ill.) on Monday announced that he will vote for Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.) to become the next secretary of State, a sign that Rubio is on a path to be confirmed on President-elect Trump’s first day of office with overwhelming support.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson backs conditions on California fire aid
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is backing placing conditions on disaster assistance for California in the wake of the destructive wildfires blazing around the Los Angeles area, a position already drawing opposition from Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Judge: DFL candidate’s election stands for Shakopee area Minnesota House seat
item.description: State Rep. Brad Tabke, a DFL Minnesota House incumbent who narrowly won reelection, should be able to begin a new term, a judge ruled Tuesday — despite a GOP election challenge centering on a pool of ballots that were cast but never counted.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Prosecutor who investigated Hunter Biden defends probes, denounces president's remarks in new report
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The criminal charges against Hunter Biden “were the culmination of thorough, impartial investigations, not partisan politics,” the prosecutor who led the probes said in a report that sharply criticized President Joe Biden for having maligned the Justice Department when he pardoned
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Utah's push to wrest control of public land from the federal government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court turned back a push by the state of Utah to wrest control of vast areas of public land from the federal government, marking a small victory for land conservation advocates who worry that similar efforts may escalate in a Republican-controlled Washington.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schools brace for clash with immigration officials ahead of Trump term
item.description: K-12 schools across the U.S. are gearing up for potential efforts to shield their students as the incoming Trump administration sets its sights on deporting millions of people.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: What Project 2025 says about the first 180 days of a presidency
item.description: Project 2025 lists four pillars as part of its plan, and the fourth pillar is an unpublished 180-day "Transition Playbook.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas 'Preview Mania 2025' set for Thursday with a nod to WWE
item.description: About 1,500 business leaders are expected to attend Las Vegas Preview on Thursday at the Las Vegas Convention Center to hear about the trends that are shaping economic growth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden announces student debt relief for 150,000 borrowers, bringing total helped to more than 5M
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it was forgiving student loans for more than 150,000 borrowers, bringing the total number of individuals impacted by debt relief in President Biden’s term to over 5 million.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Preview: Minnesota’s 94th legislative session
item.description: Less than a day remains until Minnesota lawmakers begin the 94th legislative session. And there’s a lot that is still not resolved between the DFL and Republicans, whose 67-67 tie in the House of Representatives has gotten messy.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini, Dana Ferguson, and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Despite Trump's win, 'election integrity' activists still seek sweeping voting changes
item.description: Republican voters say they trust the 2024 election was administered well, yet pro-Trump conservatives are pushing some sweeping reforms to voting systems.
item.dc:creator: Miles Parks, MPR News

item.title: Trump: Jack Smith shouldn't be allowed to release report
item.description: "Why would Deranged Jack Smith be allowed to issue a “report” on a complete and total Witch Hunt against me, strictly for political purposes,” Trump said in a Truth Social post.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Rahm Emanuel steps down as Japan ambassador ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: “Last year, I spoke at the U.S. Naval War College and found it both rewarding and reaffirming,” Emanuel said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok app a ‘national security risk,' Sen. Mark Kelly says
item.description: “I imagine [there's gonna] be a disruption in the service here, you know, starting on the 19th,” Kelly told CBS News’s Margaret Brennan on “Face the Nation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Vance says Jan. 6 participants who committed violence 'obviously' shouldn't be pardoned
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President-elect JD Vance says people responsible for the violence during the Capitol riot “obviously” should not be pardoned, as President-elect Donald Trump is promising to use his clemency power on behalf of many of those who tried on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden awards Pope Francis the Presidential Medal of Freedom with distinction
item.description: The White House commended Pope Francis for his commitment to serving the poor, as well as advocating for peace and protecting the planet.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold a federal law that could force TikTok to shut down on Jan. 19
item.description: The Supreme Court seems likely to uphold a law that would ban TikTok in the United States beginning Jan. 19 unless the popular social media program is sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: President-elect Trump to be sentenced in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In a singular moment in U.S. history, President-elect Donald Trump faces sentencing Friday for his New York hush money conviction after the nation's highest court refused to intervene.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: A sharply divided Supreme Court has rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: U.S. stock markets close to honor former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: The New York Stock Exchange and the Nasdaq will both observe Thursday's national day of mourning in a Wall Street tradition dating back to 1865.
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan, MPR News

item.title: New book alleges that someone at Fox News gave Trump's team early look at town hall questions
item.description: Someone inside Fox News gave Donald Trump's presidential campaign the questions he would face ahead of a town hall the network produced in January 2024, a forthcoming book alleges, according to excerpts reported by CNN.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, WFLA, NBC
